title: Marl & Stone Website & Commerce Proposal
summary: A draft Fufu Studio website and commerce proposal presented as a horizontal editorial deck.

blocks.title: Marl & Stone
blocks.tagline: A home worthy of the work.

slides.label: Why this project
slides.heading: Marl & Stone has outgrown the places it lives.

body: Six years of markets, wholesale accounts, and a quiet, growing waitlist. In person the pieces sell themselves - but online, the brand is spread thin across a linktree, an inbox, and a template that was never built to carry it.
This project is about giving the work a proper home: one that sells directly, tells the story behind each piece, and has room to grow as you do.

slides.label: What we noticed
slides.heading: None of them problems - all of them openings.

items.title: The catalogue is scattered.
items.body: Collections live across Instagram, a linktree, and a stock theme. The full range is hard to see in one place.

items.title: The photography deserves more room.
items.body: The product shots are genuinely beautiful. The current layout crops and shrinks them until they stop working.

items.title: Two audiences, one door.
items.body: Wholesale buyers and retail customers follow the same path today - and it serves neither of them cleanly.

items.title: The maker story is missing.
items.body: What people fall for in person - the hands, the kiln, the honest imperfection - is not anywhere on the site.

slides.label: The opportunity
slides.heading: Sell the way the work deserves to be seen.
slides.intro: Imagine a site that feels like holding the piece - editorial, unhurried, tactile - with a checkout that never gets in the way. Retail and wholesale each get their own clear path. The story does the selling; the commerce simply works.

slides.label: Recommended direction
slides.heading: The Studio Engagement
slides.intro: A complete, end-to-end partnership - sized for a brand at an inflection point, not a template swap.

items.title: Strategy-led
items.body: We decide what the site must do before we design a single screen.

items.title: Design-obsessed
items.body: Every detail earns its place - the way your best pieces do.

items.title: Built to grow
items.body: A CMS and commerce base your team can run without us.

slides.label: Likely direction
slides.heading: Where things might live.
slides.note: Directional - validated in the strategy phase

items.title: Strategy & Positioning
items.body: Goals, audiences, and the story the site needs to tell.

items.title: Brand-aligned Design
items.body: Art direction, page design, and a system your brand can live in.

items.title: Front-end Development
items.body: Fast, responsive build with the polish designed into it.

items.title: CMS & Content Model
items.body: A structure your team can update without touching code.

items.title: Commerce & Checkout
items.body: Retail and wholesale flows, set up to convert calmly.

items.title: Launch & Handoff
items.body: QA, migration, go-live, and a team that knows how to drive it.

slides.label: Process & timeline
slides.heading: Kickoff to launch, in about ten weeks.

items.label: Discovery & Strategy
items.detail: Interviews, audit, positioning, and a validated sitemap.

items.label: Design
items.detail: Art direction, key pages, and the full design system.

items.label: Build
items.detail: Development, CMS, commerce, content, and QA.

items.label: Launch
items.detail: Migration, go-live, and a working handoff session.

slides.label: Investment
slides.heading: Three ways to work together.
slides.intro: Same studio, same standards - scoped to where you are today.

blocks.footer: Every engagement follows a 40 / 30 / 30 payment schedule - deposit, design approval, launch. Not sure which? We'll recommend one on our call.

items.label: Option A
items.title: The Refresh
items.body: A refined build on a proven foundation - a strong home, live sooner.

items.label: Option B
items.title: The Studio Engagement
items.body: Our recommended, end-to-end partnership for a brand at an inflection point.
features: Two rounds of revision per phase

items.label: Option C
items.title: The Flagship
items.body: Everything in the Studio Engagement, with the extras built in.

slides.label: Optional add-ons
slides.heading: Expand it when you're ready.
slides.intro: The core project stands on its own.

items.title: Photography & Art Direction
items.body: A dedicated shoot, styled and directed for the new site.

items.title: Copywriting & Brand Voice
items.body: Every page written with you, in a voice that's unmistakably yours.

items.title: Email & Klaviyo Setup
items.body: Welcome, abandoned-cart, and post-purchase flows, ready to send.

items.title: Wholesale Portal
items.body: A gated line-sheet and reordering experience for stockists.

slides.label: Care plan
slides.heading: The Care Plan.
slides.intro: Launch is a beginning, not a finish line. The Care Plan keeps the studio close - hosting handled, monthly improvements, and priority access when something new comes up.
slides.note: Optional. Most partners stay because they want to keep building - not because something's broken.

items.title: Approve this direction
items.body: A quick call to align and answer questions.

items.title: Sign the SOW & PSA
items.body: We send both documents for signature.

items.title: Deposit & kickoff
items.body: Deposit invoice and a scheduled kickoff.

items.title: We begin
items.body: Week one of Discovery starts.

slides.label: Appendix
slides.heading: The fine print, folded away.
slides.note: Thank you for reading. - Fufu Studio

items.label: Assumptions
items.detail: Timeline assumes timely feedback within agreed review windows, availability of brand assets and product data, and a single, empowered point of contact on your side.

items.label: Exclusions
items.detail: Ongoing marketing, paid media, net-new photography unless added, and third-party platform fees are not included in the project fee.

items.label: Third-party costs & tools
items.detail: Platform, hosting, and app subscriptions, such as Shopify, CMS, and email, are billed directly to you at cost. We'll recommend and set them up.

items.label: Legal
items.detail: This proposal is a presentation of our recommendation. The signed Statement of Work and Professional Services Agreement are the source of truth and govern the engagement.
